evidence_quotes.quote: live service games like, for example, "The Crew" get their servers shut off and the buyers of that game are left with a useless product because the main features are not available anymore.
The companies should be required to release an end of support plan that gives to the buyers all the tools and instructions so that the players would be able to keep their product functional.

paragraphs.text: Many, if not all, of the major publishing companies in the videogames industry do not plan for an end of support of their products. The result is that live service games like, for example, "The Crew" get their servers shut off and the buyers of that game are left with a useless product because the main features are not available anymore. The companies should be required to release an end of support plan that gives to the buyers all the tools and instructions so that the players would be able to keep their product functional.
